stories.headline: Supreme Court dismisses contempt petitions against critics of Duterte impeachment ruling
stories.summary: The Supreme Court en banc dismissed separate indirect contempt petitions filed against political analyst Richard Heydarian, Akbayan party-list Representative Percival Cendana and presidential adviser Lorenzo 'Larry' Gadon over their public criticism of the Court's ruling that struck down the impeachment articles against Vice President Sara Duterte. The Court said the petitioning lawyers failed to show a direct link between the statements and any real threat to the administration of justice.
stories.context: The Court's July 2025 ruling voided the original Articles of Impeachment against Duterte on procedural and due-process grounds, drawing public criticism from the three respondents that prompted Duterte-allied lawyers to seek contempt charges against them.

stories.headline: House says it is aligned with Marcos on keeping 2026 SONA simple
stories.summary: The House of Representatives said it is following President Marcos's directive to keep his fifth State of the Nation Address, set for July 27 at the House in Quezon City, austere but dignified, House Secretary General Cheloy Garafil said. About 1,000 guests are expected in the plenary and a similar number outside, with no red carpet or formal gowns permitted and attendees encouraged to wear simple Filipiniana attire.

stories.headline: Marcos expands cash aid to 7.5 million households amid Middle East-driven oil price hikes
stories.summary: President Marcos announced an expanded Uplift Assistance cash aid program covering 7.5 million low-income households, or roughly 37.5 million Filipinos, through the Department of Social Welfare and Development, citing the impact of rising oil prices linked to the Middle East conflict. The package includes a one-time payout of up to P2,000 for 3.5 million 4Ps and Walang Gutom Program beneficiaries, plus monthly P2,000 aid from July to December for 2.5 million poor and near-poor households and 1.5 million low-income workers.
stories.context: The aid is a second wave under Executive Order No. 110's one-year national energy emergency declaration. The war between the US and Israel against Iran has driven up global oil prices and disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: DepEd holds first active shooter drill at Manila Science High School
stories.summary: The Department of Education held its first active shooter drill at Manila Science High School, with about 1,770 students from grades 7 to 12 practicing lockdown, barricading and evacuation procedures under a 'run, hide, fight' protocol, the agency said. Education Secretary Sonny Angara said the drill is meant to be rolled out nationwide, with individual schools developing their own customized safety plans.
stories.context: The drill follows a June 22 shooting at a Tacloban City high school that killed three students and wounded 20 others, prompting DepEd to intensify school security measures nationwide.

stories.headline: Baguio marks 36th anniversary of deadly 1990 earthquake
stories.summary: Baguio City commemorated the 36th anniversary of the magnitude-7.7 earthquake that struck on July 16, 1990, killing about 1,600 people in the city, local officials said. Mayor Benjamin Magalong said the disaster prompted the city to build disaster-resilience systems and hold regular earthquake drills that did not exist before, while Representative Mauricio Domogan said unauthorized building modifications had contributed to structural collapses at the time.

stories.headline: P3.74-million worth of shabu seized in Camarines Sur buy-bust
stories.summary: The Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency in Bicol arrested three men in a buy-bust operation in Pili, Camarines Sur, seizing about 550 grams of suspected shabu worth P3.74 million, police said. The suspects, identified only by aliases, face charges under the Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act after allegedly being found with additional bags of the drug beyond what was sold to an undercover agent.

stories.headline: Southwest monsoon brings isolated rain to northern Luzon; no active cyclone in PAR
stories.summary: PAGASA said the southwest monsoon was affecting extreme northern Luzon as of its 4 a.m. bulletin, bringing isolated rain showers and thunderstorms to Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Abra, Batanes and the Babuyan Islands, with a risk of flash floods or landslides during heavier downpours. No tropical cyclone was active inside the Philippine Area of Responsibility, though a weak tropical depression was being tracked far east of Mindanao, outside the area.

stories.headline: Alas Pilipinas men stunned by Cambodia in SEA V Cup opener
stories.summary: Cambodia upset the Alas Pilipinas men's volleyball team in a five-set thriller, 23-25, 30-28, 20-25, 25-22, 15-8, in the opening leg of the 2026 SEA V Cup in Candon City, Ilocos Sur, dropping the Philippines to 0-1 in Pool A. Joshua Magalaman led the Philippines with 21 points, but Cambodia pulled away in the decisive fifth set after an early 4-0 run.
stories.watch_for: Alas Pilipinas is scheduled to face Indonesia next in Pool A action on July 17.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes for sixth straight day over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The United States carried out a sixth consecutive night of strikes against Iran, with Iranian state media reporting explosions in Bandar Abbas and Ahvaz, as the two sides continued exchanging fire over control of the Strait of Hormuz, Al Jazeera reported. Iran's military warned that regional infrastructure would be 'crushed' if the US struck civilian sites, while air-raid sirens sounded in Bahrain and Kuwait amid the exchanges.
stories.context: The conflict, which began in late February when the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, has repeatedly disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway that normally carries about a fifth of the world's oil and gas supply, driving up global fuel prices.

stories.headline: 44 Japanese firms launch sovereign AI project with Nvidia chip order
stories.summary: Some 44 Japanese companies, led by SoftBank, Sony and Honda, launched a joint project to build a sovereign artificial intelligence foundation model, and Japan plans to purchase 27,500 Nvidia Rubin chips to support 'physical AI' development for robotics and manufacturing, Nikkei Asia reported. Japanese Economy, Trade and Industry Minister Ryosei Akazawa and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang were both involved in unveiling the initiative.
stories.context: The project is described as part of Japan's strategic push to protect its manufacturing competitiveness as the United States and China advance their own artificial intelligence capabilities.
